name: review-depth-language-footguns
author: diffscope
description: Language-specific semantic footgun benchmark pack for deeper live review runs.

fixtures.name: go-nil-interface-comparison
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/internal/validate/error.go b/internal/validate/error.go
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/internal/validate/error.go
+++ b/internal/validate/error.go
@@ -1,4 +1,5 @@
 func ValidateConfig(raw []byte) error {
-    return nil
+    var err *ConfigError = nil
+    return err
 }

expected_findings.description: Typed nil pointer is returned as an error interface, so callers can observe a non-nil error even though the concrete pointer is nil.

fixtures.description: Go reviewers should catch typed-nil values that become non-nil when stored in interfaces.

fixtures.name: python-mutable-default-arg
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/app/collector.py b/app/collector.py
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/app/collector.py
+++ b/app/collector.py
@@ -1,4 +1,3 @@
-def record_event(event, seen=None):
-    seen = seen or []
+def record_event(event, seen=[]):
     seen.append(event)
     return seen

expected_findings.description: Mutable default list is shared across calls and will accumulate state between invocations.

fixtures.description: Python reviewers should catch mutable default arguments that silently share state.

fixtures.name: rust-lifetime-dangling-ref

expected_findings.description: Unsafe transmute extends the lifetime of a reference to a local String, creating a dangling reference and undefined behavior.

fixtures.description: Rust reviewers should detect unsafe lifetime extension that returns references to freed stack data.

fixtures.name: ts-equality-coercion-trap

expected_findings.description: Loose equality allows coercion so values like empty strings or false can compare equal to 0 unexpectedly.

fixtures.description: TypeScript reviewers should flag loose equality checks that rely on coercive semantics.
